$comment: Test vectors for Unseen v1 file-transfer cryptographic paths. Frozen protocol artifact:
SHA-256(JSON.stringify(vectors)) is recorded as FROZEN_SHA256_OF_FILE_VECTORS in
freeze.ts and MUST NOT change in v1. Any change in protocol behavior (algorithms,
inputs, derivations, wire layout, AAD binding) is a v2 protocol bump.
These vectors complement test-vectors-v1.json by exercising the binary chunk AEAD path
(kind=0x01) and the AAD-binding property that prevents type confusion between control
envelopes and file chunks under the same session_key.
All hex values are lowercase. session_key shared with V3 of test-vectors-v1.json.
AEAD encrypt of a binary file chunk with counter=2, direction=0x01.
Plaintext layout: [tid:8][seq:u32 LE][data:N]. tid is the transfer identifier
(8 raw bytes), seq is the chunk sequence number (little-endian u32), data is the
chunk payload (here 32 sequential bytes 0x00..0x1F for determinism).
AAD = utf8("unseen:v1:") || 0x01 (RELAY_KIND_CHUNK) — binds chunk-kind into AEAD.
AAD-binding property: the same session_key, nonce, and plaintext under two different
AADs (aadFor(0x00) for control envelopes vs aadFor(0x01) for binary chunks) MUST
produce byte-distinct ciphertexts. This prevents type confusion — a peer cannot reuse
an authenticated chunk ciphertext as a control envelope or vice versa, even if the
wire kind byte is flipped. The kind byte at frame offset 1 must match the kind bound
into AAD or AEAD authentication fails.

$how_to_verify: Re-derive every output via Bun Web Crypto:
The test re-encrypts V9 plaintext under V9 nonce/AAD, asserts byte-equality with the
recorded V9 ciphertext, decrypts back, and verifies the chunk layout decode (tid/seq/data).
V10 additionally encrypts the same plaintext under aadFor(0x00) and asserts byte-distinct
ciphertexts to prove the AAD-binding property.
Algorithms: AES-256-GCM via crypto.subtle.encrypt with 12-byte nonce and 16-byte tag.
